title: Rootful podman in rootless podman storage.conf locations
body:
### Issue Description

I run podman in a rootless podman and the inner podman doesnt seem to read the $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf file.

```shell
podman run --rm --mount type=bind,source=$PWD/containers/storage.conf,target=/root/.config/containers/storage.conf quay.io/podman/stable podman info
```

with $PWD/containers/storage.conf being

```
[storage]
driver = "vfs"
```

but the output still contains

```yaml
store:
  graphDriverName: overlay
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1.

with $PWD/containers/storage.conf being

```
[storage]
driver = "vfs"
```

2.

run

```shell
podman run --rm --mount type=bind,source=$PWD/containers/storage.conf,target=/root/.config/containers/storage.conf quay.io/podman/stable podman info
```

### Describe the results you received

the output still contains

```yaml
store:
  graphDriverName: overlay
```


### Describe the results you expected

the output should contain

```yaml
store:
  graphDriverName: vfs
```

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I just tried running the inside podman as a rootless user, and than the file gets loaded from the home directory.
Root Podman doesn't use `~/.config/containers/` paths - so mounting to `/root` won't work. You'll want to put it into `/etc/containers/` instead.

Wouldn't it be useful to be able to override configuration in `/etc/containers`? I want to mount a config that sets only some values.

Looking into the home directory would also be more consistent.

@Luap99 is doing a rework of our config files for Podman 6, so I'll tag him in for that suggestion, but on the whole I don't see much value. `/etc/containers/` is the override location; the main config files exist in `/usr/share/containers`. Adding further overrides is easy and low-maintenance, so it may still be worth it, though.

Well as it so happen for containers.conf we do actually consider /root/.config/containers/containers.conf since 5.0

I think there is no reason not to look into the home dir, it is simpler to always to that actually

> Wouldn't it be useful to be able to override configuration in /etc/containers? I want to mount a config that sets only some values.

So that is also an issue with storage.conf, it is not actually a drop in config file. It reads exactly one file location so this wouldn't work today anyway.

But yes both of these issues will be part of my new config file design for podman 6, I will work on that in the coming weeks. In short I plan to make all our config files work similar to containers.conf + some more other changes.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
